expectations.text: Checks for AGENTS.md, context/, the nine required context files, skills directory, skills-lock.json, and memory.md if applicable
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the executor read AGENTS.md (line 38), ran bash commands to inspect context/, skills/, .agents/skills/, skills-lock.json, and memory.md (lines 39, 44). The response.md lists every item explicitly: AGENTS.md present, skills/ present with 10 directories, context/ absent (none of the 9 required files exist), .agents/skills/ absent, skills-lock.json absent, memory.md absent. All required check targets were inspected.

expectations.text: Classifies the state as setup or refresh because the context backbone is missing
expectations.evidence: Response.md states 'Mode selected: Setup' and justifies it: 'The context system is mostly absent. AGENTS.md defines the backbone, but the actual context/ directory and all 9 required files have not been created yet. This is a greenfield scaffolding job.' This matches the bootstrap skill's definition of Setup mode.

expectations.text: Does not claim the context system is healthy when context/ files are absent
expectations.evidence: The response never claims the context system is healthy. Instead it states: 'context/ — entire directory absent. None of the 9 required context files exist', 'Without context/, the agent has no project operating system', and classifies the state as Setup (not Bootstrap, which would imply a working system). No health claim is made anywhere in the output.

expectations.text: Surfaces wiring issues plainly instead of burying them in prose
expectations.evidence: Response.md has a dedicated 'Wiring issues found' section with two clearly formatted bullet points: (1) caveman-commit listed in AGENTS.md but missing from skills/, (2) imprint exists in skills/ but unlisted in AGENTS.md. A separate 'Why they matter' section explains the concrete impact. This follows the bootstrap skill's prescribed format for surfacing wiring issues plainly.

expectations.text: Does not silently create or rewrite the context files before reporting state
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the executor only wrote to outputs/response.md and outputs/manifest.json (both inside the run dir). No context/ directory was created, no AGENTS.md was modified, and no context files were scaffolded. The report recommends '/bootstrap setup' as the next action rather than performing the setup itself.

expectations.text: Provides a concise report with recommended next action
expectations.evidence: Response.md is a well-structured 30-line report with clear sections: Detected project state, Wiring issues found, Why they matter, Mode selected, and Recommended next action. The recommended next action is specific and actionable: 'Run /bootstrap setup to scaffold the full context system' with details on what it will create.

claims.claim: 10 skill directories exist with SKILL.md files
claims.evidence: Transcript shows `ls -d skills/*/SKILL.md | wc -l` was run. The response lists: architect, bootstrap, discover, impeccable, imprint, recover, remember, review, syncdocs, using-skills — exactly 10.

claims.claim: caveman-commit is listed in AGENTS.md but missing from skills/
claims.evidence: AGENTS.md was read and skills/ was listed. The executor identified this mismatch and reported it. No caveman-commit directory appears in the skills listing.

claims.claim: imprint exists in skills/ but is unlisted in AGENTS.md
claims.evidence: imprint appears in the skills listing (10 skills counted) but is not referenced in AGENTS.md per the executor's inspection.

claims.claim: The context system has no project operating system without context/
claims.evidence: Since context/ is entirely absent and contains none of the 9 required files, the project lacks the structured state backbone. The response correctly explains the downstream impact: every session starts from conversation residue instead of structured state.

claims.claim: No repo files outside the run directory were modified
claims.evidence: The transcript shows writes only to outputs/response.md and outputs/manifest.json, both within the assigned run directory. No other write operations appear in the transcript.

suggestions.assertion: Checks for AGENTS.md, context/, the nine required context files, skills directory, skills-lock.json, and memory.md if applicable
suggestions.reason: This is a broad assertion that bundles many check targets. A pass could be claimed even if only some items were checked. Consider splitting into separate assertions for detection completeness (AGENTS.md + context/) and wiring completeness (skills-lock.json + memory.md) to increase discriminating power.

No assertion verifies that the report's content is accurate — e.g., that the stated number of skills matches reality, or that the wiring issues are real mismatches. The executor could fabricate issues and still pass all assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions test the bootstrap skill's behavioral discipline well: detection, classification, restraint, issue surfacing, and reporting. The main gap is that assertions verify presence/absence of behavior but not the accuracy of the reported findings.
